# Teamster

A live AI-usage observatory — real cost, attribution, and effectiveness data from daily Claude Code work.

### Dashboards

**Overview**

| | |
|---|---|
| [Realtime Activity Feed](/d/activity-feed) | Live agent action stream |
| [AI Spend Explorer](/d/fd-ai-spend-overview-v2) | Headline spend, daily cost, lead vs teammate |
| [AI Spend Trace](/d/fd-cost-explorer-v2) | Three-facet Sankey, cross-tab, model mix |
| [Usage & Effectiveness](/d/fd-usage-effectiveness-v2) | Model fit, rework tax, cache economics |

**Work & Cost Explorers**

| | |
|---|---|
| [Simple Cost Explorer](/d/cost-by-tag-value) | Single-dimension cost breakdown |
| [Multidimension Explorer](/d/tag-stack-explorer) | Two-level hierarchy, heat matrix |
| [Work Explorer](/d/entity-cost-explorer) | Cost by outcome / work unit |
| [Outcome Accounting](/d/fd-outcome-accounting-v2) | Attribution confidence, cost per outcome |
| [Outcome Explorer](/d/outcome-cost-explorer) | Per-outcome cost drill-down |

**Operations**

| | |
|---|---|
| [System Health](/d/fd-data-quality-v2) | Pipeline health, data quality |

### Getting Started

These dashboards will populate as Claude Code sessions start using Teamster. Here's how to get started:

**1. Install the client on hosts running Claude Code:**

```
teamster install-remote user@host
```

This installs the lightweight client on remote hosts — no Go or daemons needed. The hub collects events and serves dashboards.

**2. Configure your tag vocabulary:**

Tags are how Teamster classifies and attributes cost. Set them up with the guided TUI wizard (`teamster setup tags`) or conversationally in Claude Code (`/teamster:tags`).

**3. Start a session:**

Run `/teamster:start` in Claude Code to begin tracking work with WMS outcomes and cost attribution.

---

### Explore the dashboards

Once you have data, explore these dashboards:

**Follow the money** — Open the [Simple Cost Explorer](/d/cost-by-tag-value) and change the **Tag Key** dropdown. Start with `product`, then try `work-type` and `phase`. Each dimension tells a different story. Then try the [Multidimension Cost Explorer](/d/tag-stack-explorer) to slice and dice across two dimensions.

**Trace across dimensions** — The [AI Spend Trace](/d/fd-cost-explorer-v2) defaults to product → feature → phase. Try changing Facet 2 to `component` and Facet 3 to `work-type` for a different cross-section.

**Check model efficiency** — In [Usage & Effectiveness](/d/fd-usage-effectiveness-v2), scroll to the **Model Fit** section. The heat matrix shows whether expensive models are doing routine work.

**See what spend produces** — The [Outcome Explorer](/d/outcome-cost-explorer) shows cost accumulation per outcome. Filter by `product` to compare efficiency across efforts.
